item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start to help shape a change that adds architecture and code-quality specialist helpers to Flywheel. The user knows the broad goal, but bounded-context guidance, pattern posture, and clean-code enforcement still need shaping before planning and execution.
success_definition: Starts in shaping because architecture-bearing behavior still needs definition.
Carries architecture and pattern decisions into work, review, and finish stages without adding a new visible stage.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start on a reviewed requirements doc for a right-sized bounded-context and ports/adapters change, then carry that through implementation, review, commit, and optional lesson capture.
success_definition: Starts at plan because requirements already exist.
Keeps architecture and pattern decisions visible across the remaining stages.
Preserves review and commit closure with optional spin rather than flattening the workflow.
